recap: Switzerland advanced to the World Cup quarterfinals by defeating Colombia 4-3 on penalties after a tense, goalless 120 minutes in Vancouver, with Rubén Vargas converting the decisive spot-kick despite Manuel Akanji's miss and Colombia's Davinson Sánchez and Cucho Hernández failing to score. The match remained scoreless through regulation and extra time despite Colombia hitting the crossbar through Jhon Lucumí's header and Switzerland creating half-chances through Zeki Amdouni and Granit Xhaka, while unmarked Jáminton Campaz squandered the best opportunity by shooting over the bar late in extra time. Switzerland will face defending champions Argentina in the quarterfinals on Sunday in Kansas.
